releases.description:
## Changes

### Fixed
- **Issue #94 (Lint cancellation)**: AbortSignal now propagates through all 5 fix-runner functions — clicking the status bar "click to cancel" during fix phases works as intended. All persistent Notices are wrapped in try/finally so they dismiss even on cancellation.
- **Issue #96 (Lint granularity)**: LLM analysis step in lint now respects the user's extractionGranularity setting — previously it was unconstrained.
- **Issue #99 (Thinking token bleeding)**: Three-layer defense: (1) API-level disableThinking sends thinking.type='disabled' uniformly, with 400 fallback; (2) parseJsonResponse strips think blocks before JSON extraction; (3) cleanMarkdownResponse discards preamble before structural markers. Test Connection probes and caches the result per provider.
- **Issue #86 (Frontmatter dates)**: Root cause was preamble before frontmatter (shared with #99). Fixed by cleanMarkdownResponse Layer B2.

### Added
- **Issue #103 (Delete empty stubs)**: New "Delete empty stubs" button in the Lint report modal. Skips pages with reviewed: true. No configuration needed.
- **disableThinking**: ROADMAP P3 #12 — disableThinking added to LLMClient.createMessage and createMessageStream. Test Connection probes per-provider support.

### Tests
- 549/549 passing (was 512). 37 new tests.

## Contributors

- **@green-dalii** — Code, release, bug fixes (Issues #94, #96, #99, #103), disableThinking implementation
- **@DocTpoint** — Bug reports (Issues #94, #96, #99)
- **@Linate** — Bug reports (Issue #86, #103)

---

## Installation

**Obsidian Community Plugin Market (recommended):**
1. In Obsidian, go to **Settings → Community plugins**
2. Click **Browse** and search for "Karpathy LLM Wiki"
3. Click **Install**, then **Enable**

**Manual (alternative):**
1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` from the assets below
2. In Obsidian, go to Settings → Community plugins → Installed plugins tab, click the folder icon
3. Create a folder named `karpathywiki`, drop the three files inside
4. Back in Obsidian, click the refresh icon and enable the plugin

---

## Configuration

1. Open **Settings → Karpathy LLM Wiki**
2. Pick a provider (Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, Ollama, OpenRouter, or custom)
3. Enter your API key (not needed for Ollama)
4. Click **Fetch Models**, then **Test Connection**, then **Save Settings**

See the [full README](https://github.com/green-dalii/obsidian-llm-wiki#readme) for detailed usage and model recommendations.

---

**Full Changelog**: [1.16.1...1.16.2](https://github.com/green-dalii/obsidian-llm-wiki/compare/1.16.1...1.16.2)

releases.description:
## Changes

### Fixed
- **Issue #95 (Anthropic CORS regression)**: Removed `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (1.3MB) and rewrote `AnthropicClient` on Obsidian's `requestUrl`. SDK's internal `fetch` from `app://obsidian.md` was intermittently blocked by CORS. Prompt caching (`cache_control: ephemeral`) preserved by emitting the same JSON structure in the raw request body.
- **PR #88 (lint false positives)**: New `bodyWordSet()` with `BODY_STOPWORDS` (45 English function words) gates sharedLinks duplicate candidates by body-text similarity (threshold ≥ 0.2). `scanDeadLinks` now normalizes space→hyphen in target basenames.
- **PR #87 (lowercase slugs + case-variant detection)**: `computeSlug()` now lowercases output, preventing case-variant duplicate page creation on case-sensitive filesystems. New `caseVariant` signal in `generateDuplicateCandidates` catches pages with case-colliding titles (e.g., `Unix` vs `unix`) as Tier 1 — no LLM verification needed.

### Changed
- **Settings UX: drop hardcoded model fallback**: Removed `defaultModel` from all 12 provider configs. `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.model: ''` (no auto-fill on new install). Switching providers clears the model field — user must fetch models or enter manually.
- **Settings UX: friendly fetch error classification**: New `classifyFetchError()` categorizes failures into `Auth` / `Endpoint` / `Server` / `Empty` / `Network`. Each category shows a specific Notice with the relevant action. Manual entry always mentioned as fallback.
- **Settings UX: auto-switch to dropdown on successful fetch**: After Fetch Models succeeds, the model selector automatically switches from text input to dropdown.

### Tests
- 512/512 passing (was 488). 24 new tests: 9 for `AnthropicClient` rewrite, 11 for `bodyWordSet` + duplicate detection, 2 for `scanDeadLinks` slug norm, 5 for `classifyFetchError`, 7 for `extractText` type tightening. 7 new tests for `matchExtractedToExisting` regression coverage.

## Contributors

Thanks to all contributors for this release:
- **@geovanisouza92** — Issue #95 report (Anthropic CORS regression)
- **@dmarchevsky** — PR #87 (lowercase slugs + case-variant detection), PR #88 (lint false positive fixes)
- **@DocTpoint** — multiple issue reports and UX feedback
- **@green-dalii** — release preparation and maintenance

3. Create a folder named `karpathywiki`, drop the three files inside
4. Back in Obsidian, click the refresh icon and enable the plugin

**Development build:** `git clone`, `pnpm install`, `pnpm build`

## Changes

### Added
- **LM Studio provider**: New dedicated provider option with API key optional (LM Studio runs locally but supports key-based auth). Default URL `http://localhost:1234/v1`.
- **Context Window setting**: Configurable cap on LLM output tokens to protect local models with limited context. Dropdown from 4K to 1M. Shown only for local/custom providers. Also caps truncation retry to prevent retry-timeout loops.
- **Sources normalization (Issue #81)**: 6 pollution patterns (external paths, `.md`, `|alias`, duplicates, inline arrays, empty `[[]]` links) automatically normalized to canonical `[[sources/X]]`. Lint runs the fix before any LLM-dependent phases.
- **Startup quick fixes**: The former "Startup Health Check" now actively repairs low-level format issues on plugin load. Detailed 10s Notice with cleanup stats. Default ON.

### Fixed
- **Issue #81**: YAML `sources:` field generated 3+ inconsistent formats from different code paths. Root cause was `wiki-engine.ts:646` passing `file.path` raw to `{{source_file}}`, and `normalizeSourcePath` only stripping `[[]]`. 572 files / 1616 entries cleaned on the largest reported vault.
- **Issue #75**: LM Studio HTTP 400 on batch 2+ — `source-analyzer.ts:113` had a shadow `MAX_TOKENS = 16000` bypassing the centralized `MAX_TOKENS_BATCH`. Replaced. New Context Window setting provides explicit user control.
- **Issue #76**: `TOKENS_DEDUP_RESOLUTION=300` caused "empty JSON" with thinking models (budget consumed by reasoning). Fixed: 300→1000. Also `TOKENS_QUERY_SAVE_DEDUP: 150→300`.
- **Alias language**: Replaced hardcoded Chinese↔English translation rules with English-as-linker-language and a "do NOT invent translations for established technical terms" rule. Transformer no longer becomes the non-existent "变换器".
- **Dead code**: Removed `TOKENS_PAGE_MERGE`, `TOKENS_RELATED_UPDATE` (zero callers). Removed `promptIncludesConstraints`.

### Changed
- **Settings UX redesign**: New "LLM-Wiki Status" section with inline indicators. "LLM Provider Configuration" → "LLM Configuration". "Wiki Folder Configuration" → "Wiki Configuration". LLM Concurrency and Batch Delay moved to LLM Configuration. Provider dropdown uses English names for non-Chinese languages.
- **Token budget cleanup**: `TOKENS_DEDUP_RESOLUTION` 300→1000, `TOKENS_QUERY_SAVE_DEDUP` 150→300, deleted `TOKENS_PAGE_MERGE` and `TOKENS_RELATED_UPDATE`.
- **withTruncationRetry retry cap**: Now respects the Context Window setting as `maxCap` instead of unconditionally using `MAX_TOKENS_BATCH=16000`.
- **TDD standards**: Mandatory test quality rules documented — cover all production paths, assert content mutation, re-scan for idempotency verification.

---

## Changes

### Added
- **Wiki auto-initialization UX (Issue #80)**: Wiki structure auto-creates on first successful LLM connection test. No more "Generate Default Schema" button doing nothing on a brand-new vault. Settings panel shows real-time wiki init status (✅/⚠️).
- **Wiki initialization status indicator**: Real-time health check below LLM Ready status — at-a-glance visibility into whether the wiki folder structure is complete.
- **`saveSummary` i18n**: Query-to-Wiki save dialog now uses localized summary strings across all 8 languages (EN, ZH, JA, KO, DE, FR, ES, PT).
- **`isWikiInitialized` helper**: Extracted from duplicate code in `settings.ts` to provide a single source of truth for wiki folder structure check.

### Fixed
- **Issue #80 (empty vault)**: "Generate Default Schema" button silently failed because `schema/` folder didn't exist on a new vault. Now auto-creates the folder before writing config (`regenerateDefaultSchema` calls defensive `createFolder`).
- **withRetry nesting**: Removed nested `withRetry` in truncation retry paths — reduced from max 9 calls to max 3 per client. Outer `withRetry` handles all network errors on the truncation path.
- **Truncation warning log completeness**: Warning now includes the stop/finish reason (e.g., `(stop_reason=max_tokens)` or `(finish_reason=length)`) for production diagnostics.
- **Dead code**: `promptIncludesConstraints` (zero callers) removed; `foundAliases` `Array.isArray` check simplified.

### Changed
- **`parseSSEEvents` shared parser**: Extracted to `src/core/sse-parser.ts` — pure function used by both `AnthropicCompatibleClient` and `OpenAICompatibleClient`. 11 tests covering both formats, CRLF normalization, malformed JSON tolerance, and the `[DONE]` terminator.
- **`withTruncationRetry` shared helper**: Extracted to `src/core/truncation-retry.ts` — pure policy function unifying the 3 duplicate truncation-retry blocks across LLM clients. 7 tests covering cap behavior, error propagation, and warning logging.
- **Test infrastructure growth**: +37 tests (446 total across 21 files). Includes `AnthropicClient` truncation retry tests (9 tests) and wiki initialization tests (10 tests using pure mock objects, no Obsidian runtime required).
- **Constants centralization**: `PAGES_CACHE_TTL_MS` moved to `src/constants.ts`.
- **Test setup**: Added `window` polyfill to `src/__tests__/setup.ts` so `withRetry`'s `window.setTimeout` works in Node test environment.
- **Documentation**: TDD section evolved to "Development Quality Closure" with a 9-step loop and a real TDD violation example (2026-06-02).

## Changes

### Added
- **Multi-round extraction dedup**: Non-first extraction rounds now receive already-extracted names and aliases. The LLM reliably avoids duplicates even on small models.
- **Aliases seeding**: Extraction supports optional `aliases` field per entity/concept. Pre-generated aliases seed the page generation prompt for better-quality aliases.

### Fixed
- **Cross-type duplicate prevention (#54)**: `resolvePagePath()` checks the opposite folder (entities ↔ concepts) and merges content into existing pages instead of creating duplicates. Contributed by @dmarchevsky.
- **Source analysis false abort (#61)**: First batch validation now uses `&&` instead of `||` — glossary-style sources (entities without concepts, or vice versa) proceed correctly. Contributed by @Indexed-Apogrypha.
- **Hidden TypeError on non-array LLM output**: `normalizeBatchResponse` uses typed `coerceToArray` to handle models returning non-array truthy values.
- **Alias self-pointing duplication**: Spurious aliases matching the page's own filename are now filtered out.

### Changed
- **CI uses npm for build**: Matches Obsidian's verification system exactly — Build verification now passes.
- **NormalizeBatchResponse pure function**: Centralized scattered `|| []` fallbacks into `BatchValidity`-typed normalization.
- **Prompt task 0 rewritten**: Separated field round restrictions from content requirements.
- **Three-No Principle structured**: Actionable evaluation procedures for side-effect and breaking-change review.

releases.description:
## Changes

### Fixed
- **Cross-folder entity/concept duplicates prevented (#54)**: `resolvePagePath()` now checks the opposite folder (entities ↔ concepts) when same-type matching fails. When a cross-type collision is found, the new content (summary, mentions, sources) is merged into the existing page instead of being silently lost, and the name is appended as an alias. No more duplicate pages for the same topic in both folders. Contributed by @dmarchevsky.
- **Historical cross-type duplicate detection in Fast path 1**: When the same-type exact slug match hits, the opposite folder is also checked. If a historical duplicate exists, an alias is bridged to prevent silent coexistence.
- **IngestReportModal now displays collisions**: The ingestion report modal includes a "Cross-type collisions" section listing all items merged as aliases. Previously collision info was aggregated but never displayed in the batch report.
- **Build verification**: CI now uses npm install + npm run build to match Obsidian's verification system exactly. All dependencies pinned to exact versions.

### Changed
- **Type-safe i18n access**: Added `getText()` helper — replaces 13 instances of type-unsafe `as unknown as Record<string, string>` across 6 files, making missing i18n keys detectable at compile time.
- **Reduced I/O**: Cross-type collision detection now uses in-memory path matching instead of an additional file read.
- **README Usage section**: Added sidebar button ingestion method to all 8 language variants.
- **Dependency versions pinned**: All dependencies use exact versions (no ^ or latest) for reproducible builds.
- **Tests**: Added 8 unit tests for `getText()`. Total: 173 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes

### Changed
- **Extraction rearchitected**: The full wiki page list (~200K chars for 2000+ pages) is no longer embedded in the extraction prompt. This is the single largest performance improvement since the plugin's launch — ingestion is ~80% faster for typical files, and extraction speed is now independent of wiki size. A 10,000-page wiki processes each file at the same speed as a 500-page wiki.
- **Dynamic batch limits**: Short articles complete in 1–2 extraction rounds instead of many. Progress display now shows batch counts and cumulative entity/concept results.
- **Short-content auto-downgrade**: Sources under 20K characters automatically cap extraction limits to avoid over-extraction.
- **Convergence detection**: Extraction terminates early when batches consistently yield few new items — prevents grinding through unnecessary rounds.
- **Deterministic related-page matching**: Cross-referencing between new extractions and existing wiki pages now uses programmatic slug + alias matching instead of LLM guessing — more reliable and zero additional cost.
- **Granularity descriptions standardized**: All 8 languages now use ≤ notation with correct values (Fine ≤100, Standard ≤50, Coarse ≤10, Minimal ≤5).
- **esbuild upgraded**: 0.17.3 → 0.28.0 (fixes dev-server CORS vulnerability).
- **Production build suppresses console.debug**: All debug output eliminated from end-user builds while preserved in development builds.

### Added
- **What's New section in all READMEs**: Prominent localized section with key improvements per version, TOC-anchored for quick navigation.
- **`pnpm build:dev`**: One-shot dev build with debug output preserved.
- **Lint duplicate batch progress**: Shows "Checking duplicates: batch 2/5..." instead of a static message.

## Changes

### Added
- **llmReady gating (#42)**: New users must complete Provider → API Key → Fetch Models → Test Connection before core features unlock. Connection status indicator in settings. Existing users auto-migrated.
- **Cancel ingestion & lint (#43)**: Click status bar or `Ctrl+P` → "Cancel current ingestion" to stop operations at batch boundaries. Works for both ingest and lint. Folder loop aware.
- **Ribbon icon + one-click ingest (#44)**: `sticker` icon in left sidebar. `Ctrl+P` → "Ingest current file" uses active editor file directly. 8-language i18n.
- **Lint double-nested link auto-fix**: Programmatically detects and fixes `[[[[entities/Foo|Foo]]]]` patterns across all wiki directory files. Zero LLM cost.
- **Real API model lists**: Anthropic model list now fetched from `api.anthropic.com/v1/models` instead of hardcoded. All providers use real API calls.

### Fixed
- **529 "Overloaded" not retried (#41)**: Error messages embed HTTP status codes across all client classes. All 6 retry regex patterns include `overload` keyword.
- **Double-nested wiki-links in log.md (#37)**: Three-layer defense — prompt + post-processing + integrity check. Also fixes silently-skipped `updateRelatedPage` being reported as "updated."
- **Opposite-directory stub duplicates (#40)**: Slug-equivalence matching in both LLM and deterministic stub safety nets.
- **Extraction prompt noise (#34)**: Graph-centric criteria ("wiki-link test") replaces document-centric. Bibliographic references excluded — findings extracted as concepts instead.
- **Merge prompt token waste (#39)**: `truncateMentions()` caps `mentions_in_source` at 500 characters.

### Changed
- **PageFactory refactoring**: 8 entity/concept methods unified into 4 generic methods. 563→424 lines (-25%). Public API unchanged.
- **LLM client retry extraction**: Shared `withRetry<T>` helper eliminates duplicated retry loops. Code reduced -67 lines in `llm-client.ts`.
- **AnthropicClient.listModels()**: Now calls real API instead of returning hardcoded list of 3 models.
- **Dead code removal**: Unused `language` parameter removed from `createMessageStream` interface and 3 implementations.
- **Slugify debug log reduction**: 6→2 logs per normal-path call.
- **Codebase language consistency**: 19 residual Chinese comments and debug strings translated to English.

### Test Coverage
- **113 unit tests** (+15 since 1.10.2): `parseJsonResponse` repairFn (3), `mergeFrontmatter` edge cases (4), `fixDoubleNestedWikiLinks` (5), plus supplemental coverage.

### Evaluated & Rejected
- **#38 Anthropic prompt caching**: System prompts too small (<1k tokens) for Anthropic's 1024-token cache threshold. `cacheBreakpoint` already handles user message caching — main savings already captured.

## Changes

  ### Added
  - **4-layer pollution defense system**: Comprehensive folder-prefix pollution prevention for wiki-links. L1: expanded regex at write gate catches both display-name (`[[X/Y|X/Z]]`) and path-duplication (`[[X/Xname|name]]`) patterns. L2: index purification filters polluted entries from all LLM input sources (fixDeadLink, fillEmptyPage, linkOrphanPage, buildPagesListForPrompt, resolvePagePath). L3: stub title sanitization strips folder prefixes before file creation. L4: `detectPollutedPages()` scans existing pages and `fixPollutedPage()` renames files + updates all incoming wiki-links
  - **"Fix polluted pages" button in Lint report**: One-click detection and repair of polluted pages. Integrated into Smart Fix All as Phase -1 (before aliases)
  - **Missing aliases listed per-page in Lint report**: Previously only showed a count — now lists each page individually with [[wiki-link]] format
  - **Polluted pages section in Lint report**: Shows each polluted page with its clean name suggestion
  - **Long source ingestion notice**: Files exceeding 1000 lines trigger a persistent Notice explaining that iterative batch extraction requires multiple LLM passes and may take several minutes

  ### Changed
  - **Code quality upgrade (B+ → A-)**: Removed `analyzeMerge` dead code (50 lines, zero callers). Extracted `createLLMClient` factory to eliminate duplication in main.ts. Consolidated `updateRelatedPage` prompt into PROMPTS system. Fixed `getText` type safety with `keyof typeof TEXTS.en`. Standardized~130 console logs to English. Optimized slider onChange to partial refresh
  - **Python Zen design principles** added to CLAUDE.md with 4 Key Design Decisions documented

  - **Folder-prefix path pollution (root cause)**: Recurring issue where pages were created with polluted names like `concepts/conceptsAAAA.md`. ## Changes                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Fixed                                                                                                                                                  
  - **Hardcoded English abbreviations removed from duplicate detection**: The `duplicate-detection.ts` module no longer uses a hardcoded English-only abbreviation map（MoE→mixtureofexperts, CoT→chainofthought, LoRA→lowrankadaptation, etc.）. With Phase 0 alias completion (v1.7.17) now active, the LLM naturally handles cross-language abbreviation matching through aliases — no need for rigid, language-biased rules. This makes duplicate detection work equally well for all languages, not just English acronyms.                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Changed                                                                                                                                                
  - **Lint system modular refactoring**: Split 1,675 lines across 2 monolithic files into 4 focused modules:
    - `src/wiki/lint/duplicate-detection.ts` — Programmatic candidate generation（3 signals: sharedLinks, bigram, crossLang）                                
    - `src/wiki/lint/fix-runners.ts` — 5 parameterized batch fix execution functions     
    - `lint-fixes.ts` reduced from ~790 → 610 lines                                                                                                          
    - `lint-controller.ts` reduced from ~820 → 638 lines           
  - **Centralized constants**（`src/constants.ts`）: Frontmatter keys, default paths, timing values, and tree types extracted from magic strings scattered across the codebase.                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Added                                                                                                                                                  
  - **Comprehensive upgrade guidance** in all 8 README language variants: A 5-step post-upgrade checklist covering index rebuild, Lint Wiki, Smart Fix All, parallel generation enablement, and new settings review — designed for users upgrading from versions as old as v1.2.x.                                     
  - **FAQ section** in all 8 README language variants: 11 common questions covering aliases, duplicate detection, ingestion performance, Lint freezes, manual editing, Ollama/local models, language settings, Query search, Smart Fix All order, API cost management, and safe upgrade procedures.                     
  - **`src/constants.ts`**: Centralized `FRONTMATTER_KEYS`, `DEFAULT_PATHS`, `TIMING`, AND `TREE_TYPES` replacing magic strings.
                                                                                                                                                             
  ---                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                             
  ## Upgrade Guide for Users on Older Versions                                           
                                                                                                                                                             
  If you're upgrading from **v1.7.11 or earlier**（or much older）, your Wiki was built without several major capabilities added over many releases:
                                                                                                                                                             
  **After upgrading, run these steps in order:**                                                                                                             
                          
  1. **`Cmd+P` → "Regenerate index"** — Rebuilds `wiki/index.md` with aliases for every page, enabling alias-aware search.                                   
  2. **`Cmd+P` → "Lint Wiki"** — Scans for missing aliases, duplicate pages, dead links, empty pages, and orphans.
     - Click **"Complete Aliases"** to fill missing aliases（all pre-v1.7.11 pages lack them）— this is critical for duplicate detection accuracy.           
     - Click **"Merge Duplicates"** if duplicates are found（e.g., "CoT" vs "思维链"）.  
  3. **Click "Smart Fix All"** — One-click, causality-ordered batch repair: aliases → duplicates → dead links → orphans → empty pages.                       
  4. **Enable parallel page generation** in Settings → Ingestion Acceleration（set concurrency to 3; was 1 before v1.7.3）.
  5. **Review new settings**：Wiki Output Language（v1.6.5）, Extraction Granularity（v1.6.2）, Auto-Maintenance（v1.4.0）.                                  
                                                                                                                                                             
  See the [full README](https://github.com/green-dalii/obsidian-llm-wiki#readme) for detailed FAQ covering common questions from upgrading users.

## #17 CORS Fix                                                 
                                                                                                                                                             
  Replaced `openai` npm SDK (browser `fetch()`, CORS-bound) with `requestUrl()`-based HTTP client for all non-Anthropic providers. The `requestUrl()` API    
  delegates to Obsidian's Main process (Node.js), bypassing browser CORS restrictions entirely. Also rewrote model fetching in Settings to use               
  `requestUrl()`. This eliminates `x-stainless-timeout` header preflight failures and `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` rejections.                              
                                                                                                                                                             
  **Affected providers:** OpenAI, OpenAI-Compatible, Gemini, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, MiniMax, Kimi, GLM, Ollama, Custom
                                                                                                                                                             
  ## Query UX Overhaul
                                                                                                                                                             
  - **Five-phase progress indicator** — "Analyzing Wiki index..." → "Found 3 pages: Attention, CSA" → "Loading pages..." → "Generating... (elapsed 25s)",
  with page names persisted through generation phase                                                                                                         
  - **Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to send** — prevents accidental Enter-only sends, button shows shortcut hint
  - **Stop button** — send button transforms to red "Stop" during generation; stopping preserves input text                                                  
  - **Copy button** — hover-visible on each assistant message, copies raw markdown to clipboard                                                              
  - **Auto-scroll** — chat history scrolls to bottom on new messages and during streaming                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                             
  ## Lint Stability (Large Wikis)                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                             
  - `generateDuplicateCandidates` now yields to the event loop every 200 iterations, preventing UI freeze on wikis with 1200+ pages                          
  - Page reading batched to 200 files per batch to reduce I/O contention                                                                                     
                                                                        
  ## Markdown Rendering Fix                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                             
  - Fixed stale Component lifecycle in Query (fresh Component per render, explicit disposal of old ones)                                                     
  - Added explicit CSS for tables, code blocks, and blockquotes using Obsidian CSS variables                                                                 
                                                                                            
  ## Other

  Replaced `openai` npm SDK (browser `fetch()`, CORS-bound) with `requestUrl()`-based HTTP client for all non-Anthropic providers. The `requestUrl()` API    
  delegates to Obsidian's Main process (Node.js), bypassing browser CORS restrictions entirely. Also rewrote model fetching in Settings to use               
  `requestUrl()`. Removed `openai` from dependencies.

## What's new in 1.7.11                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Mandatory Page Aliases                                                                                                                                 
  Every generated page now includes at least 1 alias (translation, acronym, or alternate name). A new **Complete Aliases** button in Lint fills missing      
  aliases on existing pages in parallel batches — improving cross-language duplicate detection accuracy.                                                     
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Duplicate Detection at Scale                                                                                                                           
  The duplicate detection pipeline was completely re-architected to handle large wikis without errors:                                                       
  - **Semantic tiering** — Tier 1 signals (cross-language matches, abbreviations, high-similarity titles) are always verified; Tier 2 signals (moderate
  similarity, shared links) fill a 15K-token budget                                                                                                          
  - **Token-budget batching** — 100 candidates per LLM call at 4000 max_tokens, parallelized with configurable concurrency
  - Removed the noisy `sharedSources` signal that was generating 62% false-positive candidates                                                               
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Smart Fix All                                                                                                                                          
  One-click causality-ordered batch fix: duplicates merged → dead links resolved → orphans linked → empty pages expanded. The Lint report modal has been     
  redesigned with a 4-layer button layout.                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Frontmatter Fixes                                                                                                                                      
  - Blank line now correctly inserted after closing `---` in both `enforceFrontmatterConstraints()` and `mergeDuplicatePages()`                              
  - Frontmatter stripped before sending page content to LLM during merge

## 🔐 Supply Chain Security Enhancement                                                                           
                                                                                                                    
  This release adds **GitHub artifact attestations** — cryptographic proof that release assets were built from the  
  source repository, preventing supply chain attacks.                                                               
                                                                                                                    
  **No functional changes** — Same as v1.7.8, now with verifiable build provenance. Your existing wiki, settings,   
  and content remain intact.
                                                                                                                    
  ---                                                             

  ## 🔎 What Are Artifact Attestations?                                                                             
   
  GitHub artifact attestations provide **cryptographic proof** that release files (`main.js`, `styles.css`) were    
  actually built from this repository's source code, not tampered with during the build process.
                                                                                                                    
  **Why this matters:**                                                                                             
  - **Supply chain security**: Prevents malicious actors from injecting compromised code between build and release
  - **Trust verification**: Users can independently verify release authenticity                                     
  - **Transparency**: Complete audit trail from source → build → release                                            
                                                                                                                    
  **Technical details:**                                                                                            
  - Uses GitHub Actions `attest-build-provenance@v1` action                                                         
  - Generates SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts) provenance                                          
  - Attestations stored in GitHub's Sigstore infrastructure                                                         
  - Verifiable via `gh attestation verify` CLI command                                                              
                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                        
  ---                                                                                                               
  🔐 Security Benefits                                                                                              
                                                                                                                    
  For users:                                                      
  - Download with confidence — verify assets came from source code                                                  
  - Protection against supply chain attacks (malicious code injection)                                              
  - Transparent audit trail from commit → build → release                                                           
                                                                                                                    
  For the community:                                                                                                
  - Meets Obsidian plugin security submission requirements                                                          
  - Aligns with GitHub's recommended security best practices                                                        
  - Sets precedent for secure plugin distribution                                                                   
                                                                                                                    
  Reference:                                                                                                        
  - https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/using-artifact-attestations/using-artifact-attest
  ations-to-establish-provenance-for-builds                                                                         
  - https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1

## 🔧 Obsidian Community Submission Review Fixes                                                                  
                                                                                                                    
  This release addresses all warnings from the official Obsidian community plugin review, ensuring full compliance  
  with Obsidian API guidelines and code quality standards.                                                          
                                                                                                                    
  **No functional changes** — All fixes are code quality improvements. Your existing wiki, settings, and content    
  remain intact.
                                                                                                                    
  ---                                                             

  ## 📋 What Was Fixed                                                                                              
   
  ### API Compliance (7 locations)                                                                                  
  **Replaced deprecated `activeWindow.setTimeout` → `window.setTimeout`**
  - Why: Obsidian API guidelines require using standard `window` API instead of Electron's deprecated `activeWindow`
  - Locations:                                                                                                      
    - `llm-client.ts`: Network retry delays (2 places)                                                              
    - `auto-maintain.ts`: Temp path cleanup, debounce timer, startup delay (3 places)                               
    - `wiki-engine.ts`: API delay, batch delay (2 places)                                                           
  - Impact: Zero functional change, improves Electron compatibility                                                 
                                                                                                                    
  ### Type Safety (2 warnings fixed)                                                                                
  **Added proper type assertion to `loadData()` result**                                                            
  ```typescript                                                                                                     
  // Before (unsafe any)                                                                                            
  const savedData = await this.loadData();                                                                          
  this.settings = Object.assign({}, DEFAULT_SETTINGS, savedData);                                                   
                                                                                                                    
  // After (safe typed)                                                                                             
  const savedData = await this.loadData() as Partial<LLMWikiSettings> | null;                                       
  this.settings = Object.assign({}, DEFAULT_SETTINGS, savedData || {});      
```                                       
  - Fixes: Unsafe any assignment and unsafe .wikiLanguage member access                                             
  - Backward compatible: || {} null protection ensures old user data loads correctly                                
                                                                                                                    
  CSS Format Consistency (4 locations)                                                                              
                                                                                                                    
  Expanded short hex colors to 6-digit format                                                                       
  - styles.css lines 74, 87, 120, 131                                                                               
  - Changed: #666 → #666666, #ddd → #dddddd, #999 → #999999                                                         
  - Why: Obsidian CSS guidelines require full 6-digit hex format for consistency
  - Impact: Visual appearance unchanged (same colors), just format standardized                                     
                                                                                                                    
  Code Quality (2 improvements)                                                                                     
                                                                                                                    
  - Removed unused parameter: Deleted _retryError in catch block (wiki-engine.ts)                                   
  - Replaced deprecated dependency: builtin-modules → Node.js native module.builtinModules                          
    - Why: builtin-modules package marked as deprecated by Obsidian review                                          
    - Solution: Use Node.js v16+ built-in API instead                                                               
    - Impact: Build process unchanged, eliminates third-party dependency                                            
                                                                                                                    
  ---                                                                                                               
  📦 Installation                                                                                                   
                                                                  
  Manual Installation (Recommended)
                                                                                                                    
  1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` from this release                                                  
  2. In Obsidian, go to `Settings` → `Community plugins`                                                                
  3. Click the folder icon on the Installed plugins tab to open your plugins directory                              
  4. Create a new folder named `karpathywiki`                                                                    
  5. Drop the three files inside                                                                                    
  6. Back in Obsidian, click the refresh icon                                                                       
  7. Find `Karpathy LLM Wiki` in the list, toggle it on                                                               
                                                                                                                    
  Development Build                                                                                                 
  ```                                                                                                                  
  git clone https://github.com/green-dalii/obsidian-llm-wiki                                                        
  cd obsidian-llm-wiki                                                                                              
  pnpm install                                                                                                      
  pnpm build                                                                                                        
 ```                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                                                                               
  🔧 Upgrade from v1.7.7                                                                                            
                                                                                                                    
  Direct upgrade — no special steps required                      
                                                                                                                    
  Your existing wiki content (pages in wiki/), plugin settings, and schema configuration are stored in your vault   
  root, not in the plugin folder. Simply replace the plugin files:                                                  
                                                                                                                    
  1. Disable the plugin in Settings → Community plugins                                                             
  2. Delete .obsidian/plugins/karpathywiki/ folder
  3. Install fresh with new files from this release                                                                 
  4. Enable the plugin — all your wiki data persists                                                                
                                                                                                                    
  ---                                                                                                               
  ✅ Verification                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                    
  All fixes verified:                                             
  - pnpm lint: 0 errors (meets Obsidian Bot requirements)                                                           
  - pnpm build: Success (new dependency system working)                                                             
  - Functional tests: No changes to ingestion, query, lint, or maintenance                                          
  - Backward compatibility: Old settings load correctly with type-safe code                                         
                                                                                                                    
  ---                                                                                                               
  📋 Full Changelog                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                    
  See `CHANGELOG.md` for detailed technical changes since v1.7.7.

## 🚀 Major Features                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Ingestion Parallel Acceleration (v1.7.3)                                                                                                               
  - **Configurable page generation concurrency** (1-5, default 1) for up to 3x speedup on sources with 50+ entities                                          
  - **Promise.allSettled error isolation** — single page failure doesn't block batch, with per-page exponential backoff retry                                
  - **Batch delay control** (100-2000ms) for API rate limit protection                                                                                       
  - Progress tracking with real-time batch completion logs                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Intelligent Multi-Source Merge (v1.7.2) — Critical Fix                                                                                                 
  - **Fixes knowledge loss on page update**: Frontmatter `sources` array now programmatically appended (not overwritten)                                     
  - **Frontmatter preservation**: `created` date preserved, `updated` date refreshed, `reviewed: true` flag protected                                        
  - **Three-mode merge architecture**:                                                                                                                       
    - New pages: Full LLM-generated content with complete structure                                                                                          
    - Existing pages: LLM performs intelligent body merge following schema-defined sections                                                                  
    - Reviewed pages: Minimal append-only mode preserving user edits                                                                                         
  - **NO_NEW_CONTENT signal**: Skip redundant updates when source adds nothing new                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Verbatim Mentions Preservation (v1.7.3)                                                                                                                
  - Source quotes in `mentions_in_source` kept in original language                                                                                          
  - Optional translation in parentheses when wiki language differs from source                                                                               
  - LLM prompt explicitly instructs: preserve verbatim, translation as appendix only

  ## ✨ Additional Improvements                                   

  ### Multi-Folder Auto-Watch (v1.7.1)                                                                                                                       
  - `watchedFolders` array replaces single `watchedFolder` — watch any number of folders
  - "Add Folder" buttons in settings with folder picker modal                                                                                                
  - Web Clipper preset: one-click adds `Clippings/` folder                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Semantic Entity Deduplication (v1.7.1)                                                                                                                 
  - LLM fallback when slug matching fails                                                                                                                    
  - Handles translations ("Tsinghua University" ↔ "清华大学"), abbreviations, renamings                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Schema Template Optimization (v1.7.3)

    - `MarkdownRenderer.render()` Component parameter

  - **v1.7.1**: Entity name translation leak fixed — names preserve source language

  - **v1.7.1**: `fillEmptyPage` "file not found" resolved
  - **v1.7.1**: Auto-maintain settings sync and startup noise fixes

  ---

  ## 🛠️  Technical Changes

  ### New Prompts
  - `mergeEntityPage` / `mergeConceptPage`: Schema-guided intelligent fusion
  - `appendToReviewedPage`: Minimal mode for user-reviewed content

  ### New Utilities

  - `extractBody()`: Content extraction after frontmatter

  ---

  ## Manual Installation

  If you prefer to install manually or the automatic update doesn't work:

  1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` (if exists) from the Assets below

  3. Copy the downloaded files into that folder

releases.description:
# 1.6.5 — Cumulative Quality Update (v1.6.2–v1.6.5)                                    
                                                                                                                                                              
  ## Fixed                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                              
  - Wiki content language: entities, concepts, summaries, and page titles now correctly output in the selected wiki language when it differs from the UI language. Root cause: Chinese instruction text in user prompts was overriding the system-level language directive because LLMs prioritize user-prompt language signals over system-prompt instructions. All LLM-facing prompts are now in English, following the standard training pattern where English meta-instructions correctly govern target-language output.                                                                 
  - `{{` double brace JSON parse failures: normalizer dedupes prefill echo before parsing.                                                                      
  - "after JSON at position N" parse failures: normalizer extracts valid JSON prefix when trailing content exists.                                                                                                                                  
  - source_title missing validation: prompt now explicitly requires source_title; code falls back to file basename if still omitted.                                                                                                                   
  - Prefill safety: both AnthropicClient and AnthropicCompatibleClient now detect and recover from prefill { being stripped by the provider.                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                              
  ## Added                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                              
  - Iterative batch extraction: analyzeSource extracts `entities/concepts` in batches of 20, preventing the max_tokens bottleneck that limited extraction from long sources.                                                                           
  - Extraction granularity setting: `Fine / Standard / Coarse` dropdown with cost labeling.                                                                     
  - Adaptive `batch_size`: when a batch response exceeds 70% of max_tokens, the next batch automatically shrinks by 25% (floor: 5 items) to prevent output truncation.                                                                               
  - Anthropic prompt caching: static prompt prefix cached via cache_control ephemeral, reducing redundant token processing across batches.                                                                                                       
  - Entity type expansion: added product, event entity types.                                                                                                 
  - Ingestion report enhancements: elapsed time display, failed-item guidance text.                                                                           
  - API Key password masking: settings input uses password field type.                   
  - Wiki Output Language setting: 8-language dropdown (English, 中文, 日本語, 한국어, Deutsch, Français, Español, Português) with custom language input for unsupported languages. Wiki content language is now independent of the UI language setting.                                                                           
  - System prompt language directive architecture: buildWikiLanguageDirective() injects a per-task language instruction at the beginning of every LLM system prompt.                                                                              
  - Belt-and-suspenders language reinforcement: finalPrompt appends a CRITICAL LANGUAGE REQUIREMENT hint at the end of the analyzeSource user prompt. 
                                                                                                                                                              
  ## Changed                                                                                                                                                  
  
  - JSON parsing: complete rewrite with dual-layer architecture — Layer 1 normalizer (fences, {{/missing { correction, "after JSON" truncation) + Layer 2 extractor (brace counting, greedy regex fallback, LLM repair).                                                                                                                       
  - Summary quality: entity/concept summaries upgraded from 1 sentence to 2-4 sentences.                                                                      
  - analyzeSource `max_tokens`: 4000 → 16000.                                                                                                                   
  - Entity extraction balance: prompt now emphasizes extracting important entities even with low mention frequency.                                                                                                                                    
  - All LLM-facing prompts converted from bilingual to English-only: granularity instructions, batch context, merge strategies, page templates, index labels, contradiction notes, and conversation summary sections.              
  - Query engine language alignment: buildWikiContext() now emits the language directive in the system prompt.                                                                                                                                        
  - Entity types reduced from 8 to 6: removed "artifact" type (too vague, produced generic low-value pages).                                                                                                                                         
  - Backward-compatible migration: existing installations without wikiLanguage automatically inherit from the language setting.                                                                                                                        

  ---                                                                                                                                                         

  ## Manual Installation                                                                                                                                      

  1. Download main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css from the assets below                                                                                    
  2. Create a folder llm-wiki inside your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/` directory                                                                                
  3. Copy the three files into that folder                                                                                                                    
  4. Enable the plugin in Settings → Community plugins                                                                                                        
  5. Configure your LLM provider and API key in the plugin settings panel                                                                                     
  6. Select the desired wiki output language in the plugin settings

## What's New in 1.4.0                           
                                                                                                                                                             
  This release consolidates all Phase 1 robustness work and passes Obsidian review bot validation.
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### New Features                                                    
                                                                                                                                                             
  - **Schema Layer** — `schema/config.md`, the third layer from Karpathy's design. Human-editable config that governs LLM operation on the Wiki (naming      
  conventions, page templates, classification rules)                                                                                                         
  - **Auto-Maintenance** — file watcher + periodic lint + startup health check. All default OFF to avoid surprise API costs.                                 
  - **JSON Output Mode** — forces `response_format: { type: "json_object" }` for all structured LLM calls, eliminating malformed JSON at the source
  - **Ingestion Report Modal** — structured summary window after each ingest: created/updated pages, failed items, contradictions found                      
  - **Hierarchical Index** — LLM-generated tree-structured index grouped by type, with automatic flat fallback for large wikis (>25 pages)                   
  - **Schema Suggestion** — LLM analyzes Wiki health and proposes schema improvements                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Bug Fixes                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                             
  - **Network Resilience**: 5-min timeout + exponential backoff retry (max 3) for transient connection errors
  - **Fault Tolerance**: per-entity/concept try-catch with auto-retry; single page failure no longer aborts entire ingestion                                 
  - **JSON Parsing Robustness**: state-machine content-quote escaping + LLM repair pipeline handles unescaped quotes, trailing commas, missing commas        
  - **Index Generation Hang**: large wikis bypass LLM and use fast flat index; `max_tokens` 2000→4000                                                        
  - **API Throttling**: 300ms delay between LLM calls to prevent connection errors                                                                           
  - **Frontmatter Wikilinks**: source file references now use `[[path]]` format for clickable Obsidian links                                                 
  - **Progress Toast**: auto-dismisses when ingestion completes                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                             
  ### Compliance                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                             
  - ESLint + `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` installed for local validation
  - All UI text uses sentence case per Obsidian guidelines                                                                                                   
  - `minAppVersion` updated to 1.4.0 to match actual API usage                                                                                               
  - TypeScript 5.9.3                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                             
  ---                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                             
  **Full Changelog**: [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/green-dalii/obsidian-llm-wiki/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

releases.description:
## Pre-release (Beta)                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                        
  This is a beta pre-release of the Schema + Auto-Maintenance features. Feedback and testing are welcome.
                                                                                                                                                        
  ---                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                        
  ## What's New                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                        
  ### Schema Layer                                                                                                                                      
  - New `wiki/schema/config.md` configuration file defining Wiki structure conventions (naming rules, page templates, category taxonomy, maintenance    
  policies)                                                                                                                                             
  - Schema content is automatically injected into all LLM system prompts for improved generation quality and cross-page consistency                     
  - New command "Suggest schema updates" lets the LLM propose Schema improvements, written to `wiki/schema/suggestions.md` for manual review            
  - Graceful degradation when Schema file is missing — existing functionality is unaffected
                                                                                                                                                        
  ### Auto Maintenance                                                                   
  - **File Watcher**: monitors `sources/` folder for changes, supports notify-only or auto-ingest modes (disabled by default)                           
  - **Periodic Lint**: run Wiki integrity checks hourly, daily, or weekly (disabled by default; skips if no source files have changed since last run)
  - **Startup Check**: Wiki page statistics on plugin load (disabled by default)                                                                        
  - Multi-layer loop protection: 60s debounce cap + plugin self-write markers + 120s write expiry
  - Cost warning banner and Beta badge in settings panel                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                        
  ## Bug Fixes                                                                                                                                          
  - Fix wiki-links not being clickable inside Query Modal                                                                                               
  - Fix MarkdownRenderer lifecycle issue (pass Modal instance instead of Plugin)                                                                        
  - Fix sentence case violations, `as any` casts, unused variables, unhandled promises, and control character regex across multiple files               
  - Fix potential ingest loop when saving Query Modal conversations to Wiki                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                        
  ## Notes                                                                                                                                              
  - All auto-maintenance features are **disabled by default** — enable in settings                                                                      
  - Enabling auto features will incur API costs; review the cost warning in settings before enabling                                                    
  - Schema file is optional — the plugin works normally without it                                                                                      
  - Version tag `1.3.1-beta` — stable release will use `1.3.1`                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                        
  ## Installation                                                                                                                                       
  Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/llm-wiki/` folder, then reload Obsidian.

releases.description:
## [1.2.0] - 2026-04-27

### Changed

**Modular Architecture (main.ts 2987 → 305 lines, -90%)**
- Extracted 9 focused modules: wiki-engine, query-engine, settings, texts, types, prompts, llm-client, utils, modals
- Clear separation of concerns: each module has one responsibility
- Plugin entry point now only handles lifecycle and command routing

**Settings Page Redesign**
- Replaced vague feature bullets with 3-step workflow (Ingest → Query → Maintain)
- Merged intro texts into a single paragraph with clickable Karpathy link
- First-time users can understand the plugin in 10 seconds

**Query Modal Polish**
- Increased height from 600px to 780px for better readability
- "Save to Wiki" button now uses Obsidian's accent color (purple)

### Fixed

- JSON parsing: added LLM-based repair fallback for malformed JSON (unescaped quotes, trailing commas)
- Frontmatter: ensure `---` delimiter at position 0 so Obsidian parses YAML correctly
- Removed dead code: empty `selectSourceToIngest()`, unused `streamResponse()`, unused imports
- Replaced brittle regex-based JSON extraction with robust `parseJsonResponse()`
- Replaced `require()` calls with static ES module imports in settings
- Fix code style issues flagged by Obsidian plugin review bot (sentence case, type safety, promise handling). No functional changes.

---

### Added

**Conversational Query Interface**
- Refactored Query Wiki to ChatGPT-style conversational Modal (800x600px)
- Real-time streaming LLM responses with visual feedback
- Full Markdown rendering using Obsidian MarkdownRenderer
  - Supports [[wiki-links]], callouts, code blocks, Obsidian syntax
- Multi-turn conversation with follow-up questions
- Conversation history management with visual scrollable display
- Enter key shortcut (Shift+Enter for newline)
- Auto-truncation when exceeding conversation limit (Notice feedback)
- Clear history button to reset conversation

**Knowledge Extraction from Conversations**
- New "Save to Wiki" button to extract knowledge from valuable conversations
- ingestConversation() method converts dialogue to structured Wiki pages
  - Summary page with conversation topic and key points
  - Entity pages extracted from discussion
  - Concept pages with relationships
- Reuses existing Wiki generation logic for consistency

**Technical Improvements**
- Extended LLMClient interface with optional createMessageStream() method
  - Backward compatible (non-streaming fallback)
- Implemented streaming for AnthropicClient (Anthropic SDK stream API)
- Implemented streaming for OpenAIClient (OpenAI stream: true option)
- Language-aware responses (match interface language setting)
  - English: "Please answer in English."
  - Chinese: "请用中文回答。"
- Added maxConversationHistory to LLMWikiSettings (default: 10)
- New TEXTS entries: 15 strings per language (English + Chinese)
- Settings UI: "Wiki Query Configuration" section
  - Input field with validation (1-50 range)
  - Hint text with recommended value (10-15 rounds)

**User Experience**
- Immediate feedback: see responses stream in real-time
- Interactive: follow-up questions without reopening Modal
- Clean UI: rendered Markdown instead of raw text
- Knowledge capture: save valuable conversations to Wiki
- i18n: all UI text and LLM responses respect language setting

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/green-dalii/obsidian-llm-wiki/commits/1.2.0
releases.name: 1.2.0 - Modular Refactoring
